title: The Best Movies at the SXSW Film Festival | The Big Picture (Ep. 55)
format: Sean and Chris get back from Austin and run through everything they saw at South by Southwest: Spielberg premiering Ready Player One, the horror trio of A Quiet Place, Unfriended: Dark Web, and Upgrade, Ari Aster's Hereditary, Bo Burnham's 8th Grade, Boots Riley's Sorry to Bother You, and a Mr. Rogers documentary that made Chris cry. Then the ones they had to miss.

segments.heading: Spielberg, Ready Player One, and the Vibe of South By

films.title: Ready Player One
films.note: Spielberg introduced the surprise premiere by saying 'we did not make a film, we made a movie.' Sean calls it goofy and uneven in places but says it works if you go in without trying to check the book's references off a list. Chris didn't see it but describes the festival-wide anticipation, with an activation and a hovering blimp-style presence around town.

segments.heading: The Horror Slate: A Quiet Place, Unfriended: Dark Web, and Upgrade

films.title: A Quiet Place
films.note: John Krasinski's opening-night premiere. Sean says the crowd went dead silent for ten-minute stretches, so any cough or sniffle in the theater landed harder than the movie itself.

films.title: Unfriended: Dark Web
films.note: A Blumhouse sequel told entirely through screens. Chris found it upsetting more than scary, and both note how much faster its screen-within-screen storytelling moves than the first film.

films.title: Upgrade
films.note: Leigh Whannell's near-future action-thriller with Logan Marshall-Green as a man who resists getting chipped like everyone else, then ends up in a RoboCop-style predicament. Chris liked that it delivers thrills without requiring a dozen prior movies of mythology.

segments.heading: Hereditary
segments.note: 1 a.m. screening, and a car accident on the way home

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: Ari Aster's debut, about a family unraveling after a matriarch's death, starring Toni Collette and Gabriel Byrne. Sean calls it deeply dark and describes 'the single scariest shot' he's seen in a horror movie in years. He also got rear-ended in a rideshare on the drive home from the screening.

segments.heading: New Directors: 8th Grade, Sorry to Bother You, and The Legacy of a Whitetail Deer Hunter

films.title: Eighth Grade
films.note: Bo Burnham's directorial debut, starring Elsie Fisher as a middle schooler caught between her online persona and real-life awkwardness. Sean calls it possibly the best movie he saw at the festival and singles out Josh Hamilton as her father.

films.title: Sorry to Bother You
films.note: The Coup's Boots Riley directs, starring Lakeith Stanfield and Tessa Thompson. Sean describes a telemarketer whose job spirals into a satire of American life, and says the trailer set off a wave of internet interest right after the South By screening.

films.title: The Legacy of a Whitetail Deer Hunter
films.note: Jody Hill directs Josh Brolin and Danny McBride in a comedy shot back in 2015-16 and headed to Netflix in July. Chris enjoyed it but thought it pulled its punches compared to Hill's other work; Carrie Coon and Scoot McNairy show up for one scene apiece.

segments.heading: Won't You Be My Neighbor? and People's Republic of Desire

films.title: Won't You Be My Neighbor?
films.note: Morgan Neville's Mr. Rogers documentary. Chris, who says he never cries at movies, teared up for the last twenty minutes and calls it a straightforward but remarkable portrait of Fred Rogers.

films.title: People's Republic of Desire
films.note: Hao Wu's documentary about performers chasing social media fame through live streaming in China. Their colleague Cam Collins, a jury member on the documentary side, called it the best thing he saw at the festival.

films.title: Fast Color
films.note: Julia Hart's grounded take on a superhero movie, starring Gugu Mbatha-Raw. Chris missed it but says everyone he talked to loved it.

films.title: Blockers
films.note: Kay Cannon's comedy starring John Cena and Leslie Mann, out April 6. Chris says nobody who saw it had a bad word for it.

films.title: Blindspotting
films.note: Starring Daveed Diggs, previously premiered at Sundance. Chris missed it at South By too.

films.title: Most Likely to Murder
films.note: A horror comedy with Adam Pally and Rachel Bloom that Chris didn't get to.

films.title: Thunder Road
films.note: Jim Cummings's feature, expanded from his 2016 short of the same name, won the festival's Special Jury Award.

The Legacy of a Whitetail Deer Hunter.title: The Legacy of a Whitetail Deer Hunter
